**Mobile Neo Tailor**  
Custom clothes, made to fit you. Designed in 2 minutes, stitched in 7 days, delivered home.

Farooq Adam1, Vipin Tyagi2

**Executive Summary**

Indian customers want clothes that actually fit them. Today, getting a custom fit means a tailor's shop: it is slow, inconsistent, and the customer does not know how the garment will look until it is finished. Trends sells ready-made shirts to a body that is never average.

Mobile Tailor brings the tailor inside the Trends app. The customer scans her body in 30 seconds using her phone camera, picks a garment that she sees on a 3D model of herself, adjusts the fit on six sliders, chooses collar, pocket, buttons and a monogram, pays, and the garment is stitched in our Bangalore micro-factory and delivered home in 7 days.

The economics work because we make to order. There is zero unsold inventory, zero markdown, and zero store space cost. At a selling price of Rs. 499 the per-piece cost of a shirt is at parity with a regular Trends shirt today; T-shirts are within Rs. 5\. The customer pays the same price for a far better garment, and every additional customisation she asks for is priced live and adds margin.

**Headline numbers**

![][image1]

***Operating intent:** One scan, one fit, one promise. Made to order, never made to stock.*

**The Customer Promise**

If you can take a 30 second scan and pick a garment, we will stitch a garment that fits your body, the way you want it, and deliver it to your home in 7 days. No shop visit. No measurement tape. No re-alterations.

• A guaranteed delivery date shown before payment, met every time.

• A fit you can see on a 3D model of yourself before you pay.

• Same price as a regular Trends garment if you only change the fit. Customisations are priced live and shown before you pay.

• A live order timeline showing the garment being sourced, cut, stitched, finished, and shipped.

**Customer Journey**

Two entry points reach the same screen. From the Ajio app, three home surfaces (banner on app open, hero card on the home feed, and a contextual ribbon on every Trends product page) deep-link the customer into the M-Tailor tab. In a Trends store, three placements (entry standee QR, fabric-bay shelf-talkers, and a back-wall scan zone) do the same. From there both paths walk through the same eight steps to a 7-day home delivery.

![][image2]

*Two entry points → one M-Tailor screen → eight steps to a 7-day delivery.*

**Live Kitchen: The Customer Watches Her Garment Get Made**

*Like a transparent restaurant kitchen, the customer sees a real photo at every stage. Trust is built into the order screen, not into a marketing claim.*

![][image3]

*Each stage shows a real photo to the customer in the order screen.*

**Outcomes and How We Will Measure Them**

*What the customer gets and what the business gets, with the metric we will track for each.*

**Customer outcomes**

| Outcome | What the customer gets | Metric we track |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Fit | A garment scanned to a real body in 30 seconds. | NPS \> 70 post-delivery; fit-confidence at checkout \> 80% (alpha) → \> 90% (gamma). |
| Choice | 3D preview, 6 fit sliders, collar / pocket / buttons / monogram. | Customisation attach rate \> 25% (alpha) → \> 45% (gamma). |
| Trust | Live order timeline with real photos at each factory stage. | Return or re-stitch rate \< 5% (alpha) → \< 3% (gamma). |
| Speed | 7 days from order to home. | On-time delivery \> 95% (alpha) → \> 98% (gamma). |

**Business outcomes**

| Outcome | What the business gets | Metric we track |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Zero unsold inventory | Fabric is pulled only when an order is paid for. | Markdown rate → 0%. |
| Zero store space cost | No shelf rent for this category; frees Trends shelves for higher-velocity SKUs. | Sq ft of Trends floor freed per store. |
| Margin uplift | Customisation add-ons priced live, opt-in. | Gross margin at plan (alpha) → plan \+ 2% (gamma). |
| Repeat behaviour | Saved fit accelerates the second order to under 2 minutes. | Repeat purchase rate \> 25% within 90 days (alpha) → \> 30% (gamma). |
| Capital discipline | One micro-factory at Rs. 8.27 Cr. | Payback under 18 months; scan-to-paid conversion \> 12% (alpha) → \> 22% (gamma). |
| Capacity | Single Bangalore line. | 60 pieces / day (alpha) → 150 (beta) → 200 / day at full run rate. |

**Operating Model**

*Two loops, one customer. The demand loop runs in the app. The supply loop runs in the micro-factory. The customer is the only person who sees both.*

**1\. Demand loop (inside the app)**

![][image4]

*Demand loop runs entirely inside the app.*

**2\. Supply loop (inside the micro-factory)**

![][image5]

*Supply loop runs inside the Bangalore micro-factory.*

**Unit Economics**

*Made to order is what makes the math work. We do not buy fabric we will not sell, we do not rent shelf space, and we do not mark down what does not move.*

| Product | Selling price | Net cost differentialvs regular Trends garment | Why |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Shirt | Rs. 499 | Rs. 0 | Higher cut \+ make is offset by zero markdown and zero store-ops cost. |
| T-shirt | Rs. 243 | Rs. \+5 | Same logic; the Rs. 5 is absorbed into channel margin. |
| Customisation add-on | Priced live in the app | Adds margin | Priced before pay; opt-in upside on every order. |

**Capex Detail (One Bangalore Micro-Factory, 200 pieces / day)**

*Equipment list as ordered. All numbers in INR, converted at cited Euro and USD rates. Total of Rs. 8,27,05,510 buys the full line, end to end.*

| \# | Department | Equipment | Brand | Qty | Price / unit | Price (INR) |
| ----- | :---- | :---- | :---- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| 1 | Measurement | In-house |  | 1 |  |  |
| 2 | Fit | In-house |  | 1 |  |  |
| 3 | Pattern | Gerber MTM Accumark | Gerber | 1 | $ 23,000 | 22,31,000 |
| 4 | Pattern | Gerber MTM Accunest | Gerber | 1 | $ 8,000 | 7,76,000 |
| 5 | Cutting | Single-ply cutter | Morgan Italia | 2 | Euro 1,40,000 | 3,02,40,000 |
| 6 | Cutting | Labelling unit | Morgan Italia | 2 | Euro 18,000 | 38,88,000 |
| 7 | Cutting | Pattern matching system | Morgan Italia | 2 | Euro 14,000 | 30,24,000 |
| 8 | Cutting | Fusing machine | Hashima-900 LFS | 1 | $ 9,500 | 9,00,000 |
| 9 | Cutting | Direct-to-garment printer | Epson F3030 | 1 | $ 51,092 | 49,56,000 |
| 10 | Material movement | Conveyor system | Eton | 1 | $ 1,60,000 |  |
| 11 | Sewing | T-shirt line | Juki \+ others |  | INR | 14,68,000 |
| 12 | Sewing | Shirt line | Juki \+ others |  | INR | 60,54,000 |
| 13 | Sewing | Chinos / pants line | Juki \+ others |  | INR | 65,84,000 |
| 14 | Finishing | Pressing chamber 534 | Macpi | 1 | Euro 35,000 | 33,95,000 |
| 15 | Finishing | Pressing chamber 565.17-8526 | Macpi | 1 | Euro 25,000 | 24,25,000 |
| 16 | Finishing | Folding line ACE901 | EPA | 1 | Euro 1,26,880 | 1,23,07,360 |
| 17 | Finishing | Macpi 321.715 | Macpi | 1 | $ 20,950 | 20,32,150 |
| 18 | Folding \+ packing | EPA-5001 | EPA | 1 | Euro 25,000 | 24,25,000 |
|  | **Total** |  |  |  |  | **8,27,05,510** |

**Machinery and Process**

*Department-by-department list of the technology stack the factory runs on. Each block is single-purpose, replaceable, and sized for 200 pieces per day. (Visualisation of this section is planned in the next revision.)*

| Department | Technology |
| :---- | :---- |
| Measurement \+ Fit | AI body-scan via phone camera, 30 second capture, 14 measurements with mean error \< 1 cm. 3D fit visualiser draws the garment on the customer's own body model in the app. |
| Pattern \+ CAD | Gerber Accumark for per-order pattern generation from the customer's scan. Gerber Accunest for nesting and yield optimisation, single-ply. |
| Cutting room | Morgan Italia single-ply cutter (single-piece precision required for made-to-order). Morgan labelling: every part is QR-tagged so the order never gets lost. Morgan matching system: stripes and plaids align across seams via projected grid. Hashima fusing machine; Epson F3030 direct-to-garment printer for monograms and prints. |
| Sewing | Juki and equivalent automats for shirts, polo, chinos and pants. Semi-automats for T-shirts and other knits, single-piece flow workstations. |
| Finishing | Macpi modular pressing line, steaming \+ ventilation chambers, 20-program touch screen. EPA ACE901 folding line, Macpi 321.715 finishing press. |
| Folding and packing | EPA-5001 automatic folding and packaging machine, then dispatch to last mile. |
| Material movement | Eton overhead conveyor, links cutting, sewing, finishing, folding without WIP piles. |

**Factory Indicative Layout**

*Single floor, 15,326 sq ft. Cutters and conveyor sit through the centre line. Sewing block on the right. Finishing, pressing and packing on the south wall. Fabric and trims store flush against the entrance for first-in-first-out flow.*

![][image6]

*Indicative layout. Final dimensions to be locked with civil partner.*

**Phase Gate Plan**

| Phase | When | Scope | Exit criteria |
| :---: | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| ALPHA | Bangalore, \<\<DATE NEEDED\>\> | 1 store. Internal staff and invited customers only. Shirt \+ T-shirt. | First 100 paying customers; NPS \> 70; delivery SLA met for 95% of orders. |
| BETA | Bangalore, \<\<DATE NEEDED\>\> | 5 stores in Bangalore. Public launch. Shirt, T-shirt, polo, pants. Manufacturing automation goes live. | 1,000 paying customers a month; repeat purchase \> 25%; gross margin at plan. |
| GAMMA | All India, \<\<DATE NEEDED\>\> | All categories (men \+ women \+ kids), all geographies. Add 2 micro-factories near demand. | 10,000+ paying customers a month. Mobile Tailor permanent in the Ajio app. |

**Decision Ask**

| 1\. Approve Rs. 8.27 Cr capex for one Bangalore micro-factory at 200 pieces a day. 2\. Approve alpha trial in 1 Bangalore Trends store from \<\<DATE NEEDED\>\>, single named owner accountable for 7-day delivery and NPS \> 70\. 3\. Approve 7-day delivery and the made-to-order, zero-stock model as the Trends standard for this category. |
| :---- |
